title: BiomeCuisine
description: Food items provide different hunger and saturation values depending on which biome you consume them in.
body:
# BiomeCuisine

Food nutrition changes based on consumption biome. Cooked salmon gains bonus saturation in cold/frozen biomes, loses it in deserts. Melons restore more hunger in jungles, sweet berries in taigas. Cooked rabbit thrives in plains/savannas. Dried kelp nourishes better near oceans.

**Features:**
- Configurable YAML for custom food-biome multipliers (hunger & saturation)
- Actionbar feedback: biome-optimal, neutral, or mismatched meals
- `/biomecuisine reload` command (requires `biomecuisine.admin`)
- Minimal overhead; checks biome only on food consumption
- No persistent storage

Encourages exploration and strategic food management across terrains.
